title: Centrapalus kirkii
intro: Centrapalus kirkii, also known as Kirk's centaury, is a flowering plant species in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Centrapalus kirkii include sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Kirk's centaury is often prepared as a tincture or infusion, and is also used as a component in various teas and medicinal tonics.
taxonomy: Centrapalus kirkii is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family. It is native to New Zealand. The plant belongs to the genus Centrapalus within the tribe Inuleae of the family Asteraceae. The classification of Centrapalus kirkii reflects its close relationship to other inuleae species. Further research may be necessary to fully understand its classification within the plant kingdom.
